speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O Charmian, I will never go from hence.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Be comforted, dear madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: No, I will not.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: All strange and terrible events are welcome,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But comforts we despise. Our size of sorrow,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Proportioned to our cause, must be as great

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As that which makes it.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: How now? Is he dead?

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: His death’s upon him, but not dead.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Look out o’ th’ other side your monument.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: His guard have brought him thither.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O sun,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Burn the great sphere thou mov’st in. Darkling stand

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The varying shore o’ th’ world! O Antony, Antony,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Antony! Help, Charmian! Help, Iras, help!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Help, friends below! Let’s draw him hither.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Peace!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Not Caesar’s valor hath o’erthrown Antony,

speaker: ANTONY
text: But Antony’s hath triumphed on itself.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: So it should be that none but Antony

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Should conquer Antony, but woe ’tis so!

speaker: ANTONY
text: I am dying, Egypt, dying. Only

speaker: ANTONY
text: I here importune death awhile until

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of many thousand kisses the poor last

speaker: ANTONY
text: I lay upon thy lips.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I dare not, dear,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Dear my lord, pardon, I dare not,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Lest I be taken. Not th’ imperious show

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Of the full-fortuned Caesar ever shall

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Be brooched with me; if knife, drugs, serpents have

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Edge, sting, or operation, I am safe.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Your wife Octavia, with her modest eyes

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And still conclusion, shall acquire no honor

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Demuring upon me. But come, come, Antony.—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Help me, my women!—We must draw thee up.—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Assist, good friends.

speaker: ANTONY
text: O, quick, or I am gone.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Here’s sport indeed. How heavy weighs my lord!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Our strength is all gone into heaviness;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That makes the weight. Had I great Juno’s power,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The strong-winged Mercury should fetch thee up

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And set thee by Jove’s side. Yet come a little.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Wishers were ever fools. O, come, come, come!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And welcome, welcome! Die when thou hast lived;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Quicken with kissing. Had my lips that power,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thus would I wear them out.

speaker: ALL
text: A heavy sight!

speaker: ANTONY
text: I am dying, Egypt, dying.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Give me some wine, and let me speak a little.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: No, let me speak, and let me rail so high

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That the false huswife Fortune break her wheel,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Provoked by my offense.

speaker: ANTONY
text: One word, sweet queen:

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of Caesar seek your honor with your safety—O!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: They do not go together.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Gentle, hear me.

speaker: ANTONY
text: None about Caesar trust but Proculeius.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My resolution and my hands I’ll trust,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: None about Caesar.

speaker: ANTONY
text: The miserable change now at my end

speaker: ANTONY
text: Lament nor sorrow at, but please your thoughts

speaker: ANTONY
text: In feeding them with those my former fortunes

speaker: ANTONY
text: Wherein I lived the greatest prince o’ th’ world,

speaker: ANTONY
text: The noblest, and do now not basely die,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Not cowardly put off my helmet to

speaker: ANTONY
text: My countryman—a Roman by a Roman

speaker: ANTONY
text: Valiantly vanquished. Now my spirit is going;

speaker: ANTONY
text: I can no more.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Noblest of men, woo’t die?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Hast thou no care of me? Shall I abide

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: In this dull world, which in thy absence is

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: No better than a sty? O see, my women,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The crown o’ th’ Earth doth melt.—My lord!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, withered is the garland of the war;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The soldier’s pole is fall’n; young boys and girls

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Are level now with men. The odds is gone,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And there is nothing left remarkable

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Beneath the visiting moon.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: O, quietness, lady!

speaker: IRAS
text: She’s dead, too, our sovereign.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Lady!

speaker: IRAS
text: Madam!

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: O madam, madam, madam!

speaker: IRAS
text: Royal Egypt! Empress!

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Peace, peace, Iras!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: No more but e’en a woman, and commanded

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: By such poor passion as the maid that milks

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And does the meanest chares. It were for me

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To throw my scepter at the injurious gods,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To tell them that this world did equal theirs

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Till they had stolen our jewel. All’s but naught.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Patience is sottish, and impatience does

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Become a dog that’s mad. Then is it sin

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To rush into the secret house of death

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ere death dare come to us? How do you, women?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What, what, good cheer! Why, how now, Charmian?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My noble girls! Ah, women, women! Look,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Our lamp is spent; it’s out. Good sirs, take heart.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: We’ll bury him; and then, what’s brave, what’s noble,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Let’s do ’t after the high Roman fashion

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And make death proud to take us. Come, away.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: This case of that huge spirit now is cold.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ah women, women! Come, we have no friend

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But resolution and the briefest end.
